post.pageSubtitle: Notes on knowledge that I have known

about.metadataDescription: About Helios Blog and the topics published here.
about.title: About
about.subtitle: Helios Blog is a personal space for software, learning journeys, and ideas worth keeping.
about.paragraphOne: Each post starts with something built, a problem understood more clearly, or a question worth following for longer.
about.paragraphTwo: The writing focuses on software engineering, tools, product design, and practical notes, with an emphasis on clarity, depth, and lasting usefulness.
about.topicsDescription: Focused categories give each post a clear home and make it easier to find again.
about.readingGuideDescription: Each post has an author-defined knowledge level and estimated reading time. They are quick orientation cues, not prerequisites for reading.
about.readingTimeDescription: Reading time is expressed in minutes and reflects the length and density of each post.

faq.pageSubtitle: A few notes about how content on Helios Blog is organized.
faq.adminPageSubtitle: Operational notes for the CMS and site appearance.

userItems.a: The writing focuses on software engineering, tools, product design, learning in public, and practical notes.

userItems.q: How do I find a post?
userItems.a: Use the header search to search titles and descriptions, or prefix a tag with #. The Posts page also offers tag, category, and sorting filters.

userItems.q: What do the post categories mean?
userItems.a: Categories separate articles, projects, notes, ideas, journeys, snippets, reviews, resources, life, gallery, and blog updates. The Series category is only a content group and is independent from ordered post series.

userItems.q: How can I share a post?
userItems.a: On a post page, you can copy the link, open a QR code, create a share image, or download the Markdown source.

userItems.q: How do Standalone and Series posts differ?
userItems.a: A Standalone post is independent. A Series post belongs to an ordered sequence, and its detail page links to the previous and next published parts.

userItems.q: Does the blog support light and dark themes?
userItems.a: Yes. The theme control is in the top-right corner and your choice is stored in the browser.

adminItems.a: The username, exactly one of a password or password hash, and the session secret are environment variables. The app does not store or manage accounts in the database.
Create a draft, choose Standalone or a Series and order, check the MDX preview, image, and tags, then publish when the content is ready.
Images live in the Cloudflare R2 my-blog bucket. The media library supports folders, uploads, renaming, deletion, and stable URL copying.

adminItems.q: What changes when I update the accent color?
adminItems.a: The color is stored in site_settings and applied globally through CSS tokens, so components update consistently.

adminItems.q: When should I inspect database tables?
adminItems.a: Use the data view to verify posts, categories, series, tags, tag relations, and site settings. Make content changes through the admin forms.

deletePopup.title: Delete {type}
